name: D2C ERP Order Pull — Shopware Order Integration
description: Pulls unacknowledged D2C orders from GET /erp/orders (oldest-first, limit=1 per page), maps them to the SCA push payload shape, and acknowledges them via POST /erp/orders/acknowledge after successful ERP posting. Authenticates via Shopware OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant (Shopware Integration). Optional Cloudflare Access WAF headers also supported.
_readme: 1. In Shopware Admin create a dedicated Integration (see _shopware_setup below).
2. Replace every {{PLACEHOLDER}} value in the 'settings' block below.
3. Import this file via Celigo → Resources → Templates → Import.
4. Open the imported Connection and verify the token test succeeds.
5. Activate the flow; it polls GET /erp/orders?limit=1 on each run.
6. Wire the 'pageProcessors' section to your ERP/NAV import adaptor.
7. The acknowledge step fires automatically after the import reports success.
_shopware_setup: CREATE A SHOPWARE INTEGRATION (required before configuring Celigo)
Shopware Admin → Settings → System → Integrations → Add Integration
Name:        Celigo ERP Order Pull (or any descriptive label)
Permissions: at minimum the following ACL roles must be assigned:
Tip: assign the built-in 'order-management' role to cover all of the above.
After saving, Shopware displays a one-time clientId + clientSecret.
Copy both values immediately — the secret is not shown again.
Use these as SHOPWARE_CLIENT_ID and SHOPWARE_CLIENT_SECRET below.
WHY an Integration and not an admin user account?
Shopware's password grant (grant_type=password) uses a non-standard JSON body
that Celigo's native OAuth2 connector cannot produce. Shopware Integrations use
the standard client_credentials grant, which Celigo's HTTP connector handles
natively via the tokenConfig block below — no custom workarounds needed.

_comment_auth: AUTH: OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant via Shopware Integration.
Celigo's HTTP connector 'token' auth type handles this: on each flow run
Celigo POSTs the tokenConfig body to tokenURI and caches the returned
Bearer token until expires_in seconds have elapsed.
Celigo UI equivalent (if configuring manually instead of importing):
_comment_auth_fallback: FALLBACK — password grant (interim use only, before Integration is created)
If a Shopware Integration has not been created yet and you need to test
connectivity immediately, replace tokenConfig.body above with:
IMPORTANT: The password grant uses an admin account, which is a security
anti-pattern for service integrations. Migrate to an Integration (above)
as soon as the Shopware backend is configured.
_comment_cf: Required when shopwareUrl is a public staging endpoint behind CF WAF.
Remove both headers if connecting to an internal / VPN-only URL.

exports.name: Pull unacknowledged D2C orders (limit=1)
_comment: GET /erp/orders returns orders where customFields.erpSyncedAt is absent,
ordered oldest-first. Using limit=1 processes one order per Celigo run —
safe for testing and for pipelines that need per-order acknowledgement.
Increase limit (max 200) for batch processing once the flow is validated.

_comment_pagination: Cursor-based pagination. The API returns page.nextCursor when more
unacknowledged orders exist. Celigo follows the cursor automatically.
With limit=1 this produces exactly one record per flow run.

_comment_output: Each record yielded to page processors is one Order object.
Key fields available for ERP mapping — see field-mapping section below.

transform._comment: Optional pre-processor: extract the order id for use in the acknowledge step.

pageProcessors._comment: Step 1 — ERP/NAV import. Replace with your actual NAV / ERP import adaptor.

_comment: ERP-origin fields not available from the shop — must be set here
BEFORE pushing to SCA. These come back from NAV after posting:
invoiceDate  — NAV sets this when the invoice is created
customerNo   — ASAM customer master number (NAV lookup by email)
incoterm     — if required, set from NAV customer/order defaults
lineNetAmt   — net amount per line (NAV calculates from tax rates)
lineVat      — VAT amount per line (NAV calculates)

pageProcessors._comment: Step 2 — SCA push. Fires after NAV confirms the order is posted.
_comment_mapping: Field mapping: Order Integration API → SCA payload (OrderExport-ERP-SCA.yaml)
invoiceDate          navOrderId.invoiceDate          ERP origin — from NAV post
invoiceNo            navOrderId.invoiceNo            ERP origin — from NAV post
billingAddress.*     data.billingAddress.*           direct (field name map below)
customerNo         (NAV customer master)           ERP origin — NAV lookup
lineItems[].type     data.lineItems[i].type          map to ASAM product type

pageProcessors._comment: Step 3 — Acknowledge. Marks the order as ERP-synced and records the ERP's own order id; removes it from the pull queue.
pageProcessors.name: Acknowledge order as ERP-synced
_comment_erp_order_id: data.navOrderId is the ERP order number returned by the NAV import step
It is stored in Shopware as customFields.erpOrderId alongside erpSyncedAt.
If NAV did not return an order id (error path), omit the erpOrderIds key
(or omit the mapping entry) — the API rejects empty values with 422,
so guard with a Celigo conditional or preSavePage hook as needed.
_comment_idempotency: POST /erp/orders/acknowledge is idempotent: already-synced order IDs
are returned in the 'alreadySynced' array without an error.
Safe to retry if the Celigo step fails before recording the result.

flows.name: D2C → ERP Order Pull (limit=1)
flows.description: Polls the D2C Shopware ERP pull queue, processes one order per run through NAV posting and SCA push, then acknowledges it.
_comment_scheduling: Recommended schedule: every 5 minutes for near-real-time order processing.
Increase limit on the export to 50–200 once the flow is validated to
batch-process backlog faster. The acknowledge step batches up to 500 IDs.
